response.step_by_step_log: 1. Analyzed video and identified Minecraft gameplay by Ninomae Ina'nis. 2. Identified repetitive singing segments throughout the video, which were determined to be an improvised variation of 'Lava Chicken' (originally by Jack Black). 3. Used the search tool to verify the origin of the 'Chicken' meme song in Minecraft contexts, confirming 'Lava Chicken' by Jack Black as the source material. 4. Created scene mapping based on activities: inventory organization, farming, talking, and singing. 5. Populated JSON with identified metadata and full lyrics.
response.global_summary: Ninomae Ina'nis plays Minecraft, organizing her inventory and farming pumpkins before singing an improvised version of the 'Lava Chicken' song popularized by Jack Black.

scenes.description: Ninomae Ina'nis organizes her Minecraft inventory, placing a Shulker Box and discussing her goals for the session, specifically mentioning she wants to find chickens.

The performer begins singing a repetitive, improvised version of 'Lava Chicken' while continuing to farm and explore the area in the game. The song is clearly derived from the 'Lava Chicken' song by Jack Black.
scenes.reference_lyrics_og:
La-la-la-lava, ch-ch-ch-chicken
Steve's lava chicken, yeah, it's tasty as hell
Ooh, mamacita, now you're ringing the bell
Crispy and juicy, now you're havin' a snack
Ooh, super spicy, it's a lava attack

scenes.description: The singing pauses briefly as the performer speaks to the chat, saying: 'Listen closely guys, you gotta hear the go-go-go...'
The performer resumes singing the repetitive 'Chicken' song as she continues to explore the woods, successfully finding chickens. The improvised singing continues to the end of the video segment.
